segments.title: Bilgepumps on frigate procurement traps
segments.summary: A Bilgepumps Christmas discussion uses Constellation, LCS, Type 31, Type 26, and Korean shipbuilding examples to frame why modern frigate programs drift.
segments.body: The discussion captures the episode's opening procurement theme. The panel starts from why the United States chose Constellation after LCS, then broadens into requirements growth, shipyard and foreign-design options, role discipline, and the risk of turning a frigate into a smaller destroyer.

evidence.note: The panel frames the special around why navies struggle to procure frigates, then uses Constellation and the LCS aftermath as the first case.
The discussion moves to South Korean shipbuilding options and argues that future frigate and destroyer programs need room for command facilities and group-core roles.
The Type 31, Type 26, and Constellation section links flexible space, weight growth, baby-Burke requirements, and the need for a true ASW frigate.
